name: Atlassian MCP Server (Jira + Confluence)
what_it_does: Popular MCP server integrating both Jira and Confluence — enabling AI agents to create and query Jira issues, manage sprints, read and write Confluence pages, search across both platforms, and automate Atlassian-powered development workflows.
use_cases: Creating and updating Jira issues from development workflow agents
Querying sprint progress and backlog from project management agents
Reading Confluence documentation for context-aware coding agents
Searching Jira and Confluence for relevant information from research agents
Triaging bugs and creating tickets from monitoring/alerting agents
Updating project documentation from DevOps pipeline agents
not_for: Teams using Linear, Asana, or other issue trackers
Teams without Atlassian Cloud or Data Center licenses
best_when: An agent needs to interact with Jira issues or Confluence pages — especially in enterprise development teams using Atlassian's toolchain.
avoid_when: You're using Linear, GitHub Issues, or another issue tracker.

auth.notes: Atlassian API tokens (user-level) for Cloud. OAuth2 for apps. Basic auth for Data Center. Jira and Confluence use separate tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Jira Free: up to 10 users, Confluence Free: up to 10 users
pricing.notes: Atlassian Cloud pricing per user. Data Center is perpetual license. MCP server is community open source.

performance.rate_limits: Jira API: 10 requests/second; Confluence API: similarly rate limited

known_agent_gotchas: Jira Cloud vs Data Center have different API paths and auth mechanisms
JQL (Jira Query Language) knowledge required for effective issue queries
Confluence page IDs and space keys required for page operations
Custom fields in Jira have UUID field IDs — discovery required
Rate limits can be hit during bulk operations — agents must throttle
API tokens are user-scoped — impersonation not possible without OAuth apps
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API tokens user-scoped. OAuth2 for app-level access. FedRAMP, SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA. Enterprise SSO with SCIM.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. User-scoped API tokens. OAuth2. FedRAMP, SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA. Enterprise SSO.
